items: grow on: if sth grows on you, you start to like it more
name after: give sb or sth the same name as sb or sth else
drown out: prevent a sound from being heard by making a louder noise
put on: hold; perform (a show, play, etc)
fall for: believe that a trick or joke is true
show off: try to attract people's attention and make them admire you (usually used negatively)
get along (with): have a good relationship (with)
take after: look or behave like an older relative
go down (as): be remembered for having done something
take off: become successful or popular very fast
fun: have/be fun; make fun of sb
home: make yourself at home; be/stay at home; go/get/leave home; make your way home
impression: give sb the impression; do an impression of sb; have the impression; make an impression (on sb)
joke: joke about sth/doing; joke with sb; tell/make/hear/get/understand a joke
laugh: laugh at/about sth/sb; laugh out loud; roar with laughter; have a laugh
part: take part in sth/doing; be a part of sth; part with sth; have a part (in a play, etc)
party: have/throw a party (for sb); give sb a party; go to a party; dinner/birthday/etc party
play: play a part/role (in sth); play with sth/sb; play sth; have a part/role to play (in sth); be/act/star in a play; watch/see a play
queue: join a queue; in a queue; queue up; stand/wait in a queue
show: put on a show; show appreciation (for sth/sb); show sth to sb; show sb sth; on show; steal the show; TV/radio/quiz/game show; show business
voice: in a low/deep/high/etc voice; have a good/bad voice; voice an opinion (about sth)
like sth/doing; like to do; be like sth/doing; be like sb (to do)
promise to do; promise sb (sth); promise that
prove to do; prove sth (to sb)
happy to do sth; happy for sb (to do sth); happy about sth/doing
vary → (in)variable, (in)variably, varying, varied, various(ly), variation, variety

items.sentence: I've got a/an ...... for the school play tomorrow. I really hope I get a main part!
Did you read that ...... of the new Lloyd-Rice musical? It said it was awful!
I'm going to stay in and ...... TV tonight.
We really ...... ourselves at the party! Thanks so much for inviting us!
Ellie does a/an ...... each week for the local newspaper. It's usually quite funny.
The water park we went to yesterday was fantastic, but the entrance ...... was a bit expensive.
Vanilla Sky ...... Tom Cruise, Penelope Cruz and Cameron Diaz.
When you're learning to play a musical instrument, it's important to ...... for an hour or two every day.
The play wasn't supposed to be a comedy, but everyone started laughing when some of the ...... fell over and hit one of the actors.
Louis de Bernières ...... Captain Corelli's Mandolin, which is set on the Greek island of Cephalonia during the Second World War, was an international bestseller.
Have you ...... the joke about the guy who fell off a 20-metre-high ladder? He was fine - he fell off the bottom rung!
Being the conductor of a large ...... is an extremely demanding job. You have to make sure about a hundred musicians are playing in time.

sections.title: B: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: My little brother, Carl, takes (1) ...... our dad in that they both like to sing.

The difference is that Dad's got quite a good voice. Carl can't sing at all! This doesn't stop him, though. He's always singing. He starts as soon as he wakes up, and doesn't stop until he drops (2) ...... at night.
I usually get along very well (3) ...... Carl, but he can be a bit annoying when he starts showing (4) .......
You can count (5) ...... him to do this whenever we have guests.
My mum and dad will be talking to some friends, and then suddenly the conversation will be completely drowned (6) ...... by Carl singing at the top of his lungs.
The other day, I told him he should be on the radio. For a second he fell (7) ...... it, until I told him that if he was on the radio, we could turn it off!

sections.title: C: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Marlon Brando will go down in history as one of the greatest actors of all time.
Our class is going to ...... on a play at the end of term.
I didn't really like Madonna's new single when it came out, but it's starting to ...... on me now.
Did you know that the sandwich is ...... after the Earl of Sandwich?
The band have really ...... off since appearing on the Video Music Awards show last month.
The Eurovision Song Contest is ...... round again soon. Shall we organise a Eurovision party?
I felt completely ...... down when he refused to give me his autograph. I'm never going to buy another one of his CDs!
I hope you're joking with / about quitting your job!
Come in! Make / Have yourself at home.
Having taken part in / of several amateur karaoke competitions, Daniel was determined to get himself a recording contract.
Have fun / funny at the concert tonight, won't you?
What's wrong with boys playing with / in dolls?
The audience gave / showed their appreciation for the cast by giving them a standing ovation.
We're thinking of having / giving Dad a surprise 40th birthday party.
Voicing / Speaking your opinion on a live TV or radio discussion programme is not as easy as it sounds.

sections.title: E: Error correction (extra words)
sections.instructions: Circle the extra word in each sentence.

items.sentence: What time did you get to home last night?
You shouldn't make the fun of Tim just because he's smaller than you.
A number of contemporary art installations are on the show at the moment at the Zwyborg Gallery.
Didn't you find out that joke funny?
I guess we'll have to join up the queue.
Everyone sat there in the silence as the comedian stumbled through his routine.
It's not often I laugh out loud during a movie, but There's Something About Mary had me roaring out with laughter the whole time.
I'm never going to be part with this first edition of Dickens's Great Expectations.
I have the impression of Sasha's thinking about giving up ballet classes.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
Was the concert fun? (yourself)
Did ...... the concert?

I'm sure Kelly will do well in the talent contest. (bound)
Kelly ...... well in the talent contest.

Don't worry about your costume; concentrate on learning your lines! (instead)
Concentrate on learning your lines ...... about your costume!

We got there early and so we didn't have to queue. (avoid)
We managed ...... there early.

Jason said he was sorry that he hadn't invited us to his party. (apologised)
Jason ...... us to his party.

I don't mind if you give me the money for the tickets later. (happy)
I ...... give me the money for the tickets later.

Doug isn't usually late. (like)
It ...... be late.

Sean plays the guitar very well. (talented)
Sean ...... the guitar.

You shouldn't go backstage without a pass. (supposed)
You ...... backstage without a pass.

I'll meet you outside the theatre at eight whatever happens,' said Dan to Kyle. (promised)
Dan ...... outside the theatre at eight.

I can't understand why he won the award for best video. (deserved)
I don't think ...... the award for best video.

In the end, it was a thoroughly enjoyable evening. (proved)
In the end, it ...... a thoroughly enjoyable evening.

I can't remember who you said had won. (say)
Who ...... the winner?

sections.title: G: Word formation (text)
sections.instructions: Complete the text by changing the form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: Despite the (1) ...... (SUGGEST) by many critics that reality TV would be a very short-lived phenomenon, it is still increasing in (2) ...... (POPULAR).
Several reality shows are (3) ...... (CURRENT) watched by millions of viewers each night, and it's clearly a form of (4) ...... (ENTERTAIN) that's here to stay.

Indeed, a number of people have become (5) ...... (FAME) because of their (6) ...... (INVOLVE) in reality TV shows, and have gone on to have successful careers as singers, (7) ...... (ACT) or TV presenters.
We can watch with (8) ...... (EXCITE) each night the 'goings-on' in the house (or jungle, or bar, or school of arts).
The (9) ...... (CONVERSE) between the contestants are interesting so we don't get (10) ...... (BORE) and there are frequently (11) ...... (VARY) conflicts, which provide further (12) ...... (AMUSE).
If the contestants have to give some kind of (13) ...... (PERFORM) at the end of the week, that's even more appealing.
But there's a (14) ...... (SAY) in the TV industry: 'it's all in the editing' and this is especially true of reality shows.
